title: AB 1646 part of Assemblymember Muratsuchi’s “California Refinery Jobs and Safety Action Plan,” which includes five Assembly bills to improve public safety at all California refiner
prompt_preview:

Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 1646 requires that a refinery’s risk management plan be available to the public.",
    "AB 1646 calls for the creation and maintenance of a publicly accessible community alert plan.",
    "The community alert plan may utilize notifications such as reverse 911, text, and email PSAs.",
    "AB 1646 requires the maintenance of a public alert system including alarms and sirens.",
    "The South Bay is described as a very dense urban area where refinery accidents could cost many thousands of lives.",
    "AB 1645 will ban the use of Hydrofluoric Acid at California refineries.",
    "AB 1647 will require refineries to install and maintain air quality monitors at both fence-line and in the community.",
    "Refineries will be required to publicly report the readings from air quality monitors in real-time.",
    "AB 1648 will increase the number of state refinery inspectors at the California Division of Occupational Safety and Health.",
    "A budget request has been made in conjunction with AB 1648."
  ]
}
```
